response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile full-stack engineer with strong craftsmanship (TDD, testing tools, workshops), but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. Large-scale distributed systems exposure is limited to roughly two years at Sociomantic (in DLang, not C++), and there is no evidence of leading technical strategy for a compute/infrastructure platform or formally mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in a leadership capacity.
strengths: Broad, hands-on engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks, and active knowledge sharing via workshops and talks
Some high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8 years of C++ experience minimum (~1 year of professional C/C++)
Career is centered on web/product development (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang), not cloud compute infrastructure or hardware/storage architecture
No formal technical-leadership track record leading platform strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps teams

key_factors.factor: Strong testing culture and knowledge-sharing skills
